# Copilot Instructions — Juno Feedback Digest

You are assisting with the **Juno Feedback Digest** project — a fictional Dynatrace internal capability being developed as part of the AI-First Hands-On Workshop.

---

## Project Summary

The Juno Feedback Digest is an automated weekly digest system that:

- **Collects** developer feedback from Slack channels, support tickets, and survey responses
- **Analyses** that feedback for trends, sentiment, and recurring themes
- **Produces** concise digest summaries with action items for product teams at Dynatrace

This project mirrors real Juno DevRel work: processing developer feedback at scale to inform product decisions.

---

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `knowledge-base/project-context.md` | Full project background — read this first |
| `knowledge-base/personas.md` | The 3 user personas this system serves |
| `knowledge-base/sample-feedback.md` | 30 fictional feedback entries representing real developer input |
| `docs/prd-draft.md` | The Product Requirements Document (partially complete) |
| `docs/architecture-sketch.md` | Skeleton data flow — open for contribution |

---

## How to Help

- When asked to **complete a document**, read the existing content first and preserve the tone and structure
- When asked to **analyse feedback**, use `knowledge-base/sample-feedback.md` as your source of truth
- When asked to **write user stories**, reference the personas in `knowledge-base/personas.md`
- When asked to **propose an architecture**, align with the goals in the PRD and project context
- When drafting **announcements or communications**, keep the tone friendly, concise, and Dynatrace-internal

---

## Tone and Style

- Audience: Dynatrace R&D engineers, product managers, and developer advocates
- Tone: Practical, direct, and collaborative — avoid marketing fluff
- Use clear headings, bullet points, and short paragraphs
- When writing PRD content, follow the structure already established in `docs/prd-draft.md`

---

## Constraints

- This is a **fictional project** used for learning purposes — do not reference real Dynatrace systems unless asked
- All feedback in `knowledge-base/sample-feedback.md` is **fictional** — treat it as real for the purposes of analysis tasks
- Do not make up personas — use only those defined in `knowledge-base/personas.md`
